methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Planted Solar is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Eric Brown that has raised $32M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector comparables (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside a broader universe of 9 private climate-tech companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $32M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $64M. Peer-median analysis references three direct comparables with valuations ranging from $110M to $200M, yielding a median of $150M. The disparity reflects the stage-progression method's reliance on capital raised—a lower-bound proxy—versus peer multiples that incorporate market sentiment and product maturity.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a valuation range of $54M to $172M, with a median of $107M. The width of this range reflects material uncertainty around Planted Solar's competitive positioning relative to named peers, technology de-risking, and whether the company's metrics (capital efficiency, revenue growth, unit economics) track closer to the lower or upper quartile of the climate-energy peer set. Resolution of valuation within this range depends on commercial traction and burn rate relative to peer benchmarks.
